subject: Packer local-docker support for ARM64
commit_message:
Packer local-docker support for ARM64

Ansible playbook local-docker.yaml was fixed to install
arm64 specific binaries of docker-compose and golang
into arm64 images created by packer.

Change-Id: Ief9fbbeed5847bb2c7842d906dcf9cb4a4f34453
Issue-ID: CIMAN-229
Signed-off-by: Martin Klozik <martin.klozik@tieto.com>

Patch Set 1:

Jenkins failure seems to be unrelated to my patch. There is an authorization to the vexxhost, which prevents templates verification:

{CODE}
2019/03/27 17:15:24 Preparing build: vexxhost
2019/03/27 17:15:25 Build 'vexxhost' prepare failure: 1 error(s) occurred:

* Authentication failed
2019/03/27 17:15:25 ui error: Template validation failed. Errors are shown below.
2019/03/27 17:15:25 ui error: Errors validating build 'vexxhost'. 1 error(s) occurred:

* Authentication failed
2019/03/27 17:15:25 [INFO] (telemetry) Finalizing
{CODE}

Patch Set 1:

Let me check.. The verify job basically runs a small sanity check on all available templates and the centos one seems to be having the issues. 
I'll investigate on this.

Patch Set 1:

> Let me check.. The verify job basically runs a small sanity check
 > on all available templates and the centos one seems to be having
 > the issues.
 > I'll investigate on this.

Hi Jessica,

were you able to find the root cause of packer verify job failure? I've compared logs from successful run (100) from February, but I was not able to find any issue. Job configuration was changed a bit, but again I've not found any issue. I've also tried another temp patch (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/83558/) with no changes and with removal of centos vars, to ensure it is not specific to the image used by this template. However it failed again with the same authentication cause.

Could you doublecheck CLOUDENV settings used by packer verify job, that it contains valid vexxhost details and credentials?

Thanks, Martin
Patch Set 2: Patch Set 1 was rebased

Change has been successfully merged by Jessica Wagantall

FAILURE of ARM64 jobs is expected - there is a bug, which installs 32bit version of docker into ARM64 image. There is an review (on hold due some ongoing discussion) to solve this issue at:

https://gerrit.linuxfoundation.org/infra/#/c/15072/

The x86 job has failed because of 90 minutes timeout. As far as I can see in the log, everything was working ok, until job was forcibly interrupted - probably infrastructure was slow, so it took longer than expected.
